# Pokémon Mouse Follower

> A free, open-source macOS menu-bar app: a pixel-art Pokémon character walks
> across your screen and follows your mouse cursor. It keeps a small distance
> and moves with its own speed and acceleration, and falls asleep when idle.

## Key facts
- Platform: macOS 13 (Ventura) or later; Apple Silicon and Intel (universal build).
- Price: Free and open source.
- Category: Desktop pet / cursor companion / menu-bar utility for macOS.
- Characters: 251 Pokémon (Generations 1 and 2), plus alternate-color sprites for 124 of them.
- Behavior: walk → idle → sleep; wakes and follows when the cursor moves.
- No Dock icon (menu-bar only); transparent click-through overlay; multi-monitor.
- Customizable: character, distance from cursor, max speed, size, sleep delay, alt color, ground shadow, launch at login. Live character preview with previous/next arrows and a random button.
- Localized in English, Korean, and Japanese (UI and Pokémon names).

## Install
- Download the .dmg from the Releases page, drag the app into Applications, and launch it. No developer tools required.

## Links
- Home: https://limfull.github.io/pokemon-mouse-follower/
- Download (Releases): https://github.com/LimFull/pokemon-mouse-follower/releases/latest
- Source code: https://github.com/LimFull/pokemon-mouse-follower

## Attribution
- Sprites: PMD Sprite Collab (https://sprites.pmdcollab.org/). Community-made pixel animations.
- Non-commercial personal fan project. Pokémon © Nintendo / Creatures Inc. / GAME FREAK inc.
